question_text: The eight planets of our Solar System can be divided into two groups. Name these groups and give an example of a planet in each group.
correct_answer: Terrestrial (rocky) planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars; Gas giants: Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
explanation: The inner planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars) are small, rocky, and dense—called terrestrial planets. The outer planets (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) are large, made mostly of gases, and have no solid surface—called gas giants.
